articles.title: Dollar Advances as Stocks Slip and Crude Prices Surge
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Tuesday rose by +0.15%. Tuesday's stock slump boosted liquidity demand for the dollar. Also, higher crude oil prices on Tuesday increased inflation expectations, a hawkish factor for Fed policy, and a positive factor for the dollar. The dollar maintained its gains after the Conference...

articles.title: Bank of Canada neutral hold (2.25%) – USD/CAD rallies to 1.37 – Press Conference coming up
articles.expand: Bank of Canada and USD/CAD update: The Bank of Canada holds rates steady, citing a soft economic outlook based on a $75 oil assumption. With WTI Crude topping $100 and boosting Canada's national income, the BoC may soon pivot hawkish. As USD/CAD rallies to 1.37, explore our two-timeframe technical analysis.

articles.title: BoC Governor Macklem speaks on outlook after keeping rates unchanged
articles.expand: Tiff Macklem is due to face the press shortly, a key moment that should give markets a clearer sense of the central bank’s thinking. The appearance follows the widely expected decision by the BoC to hold the policy rate steady at 2.25%.

articles.title: Gold holds near one-month lows as Oil-driven inflation and US-Iran tensions weigh
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) trades on the back foot on Wednesday, hovering near one-month lows ahead of the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) monetary policy announcement due later in the American session at 18:00 GMT. At the time of writing, XAU/USD is trading around $4,523, down nearly 4% so far this week.

articles.title: How and why all the Bank of Canada forecasts changed in the latest MPR
articles.expand: Here are the changes in today's MPR: GDP growth (annual) Essentially a wash on growth. The Bank explicitly states "the impact of higher global oil prices on economic activity is small." Government spending got revised up (provincial budgets), residential investment got revised down (affordability still biting). Net-zero. CPI inflation (annual) This...

articles.title: USDCAD moves to new session highs. Extends above 1.3700 and looks toward 1.3715
articles.expand: The USDCAD is pushing to new session highs after the Bank of Canada left rates unchanged, with the central bank signaling growing concern over a softening labor market. Inflation is expected to drift toward 3% in the near term before easing back early next year—assuming oil prices cooperate. That assumption on oil is not helping today. Crude oil is...

articles.title: GBP/USD falls as US Dollar gathers strength ahead of Fed interest rate decision
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair fell toward the 1.3480 price region on Wednesday as the US Dollar (USD) continued to gain strength ahead of the last Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate decision led by Chair Jerome Powell.

articles.title: BoC Holds at 2.25% as Oil-Driven Inflation Seen Temporary Despite Upgraded Outlook
articles.expand: Bank of Canada left its policy rate unchanged at 2.25%, maintaining a cautious stance as it navigates the inflation impact of the Middle East conflict. While headline inflation is rising, policymakers signaled they are prepared to look through the immediate energy-driven shock, focusing instead on whether it feeds into broader and more persistent p...

articles.title: Breaking: Bank of Canada matches consensus, keeps its hands steady
articles.expand: As widely anticipated, the Bank of Canada (BoC) left its policy rate unchanged at 2.25% on Wednesday. The focus now turns to Governor Tiff Macklem’s press conference at 14:30 GMT, where markets will be looking for further insight into today’s decision and clearer guidance on the policy outlook.

articles.title: EUR/GBP: Political uncertainty drives risk premium – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank’s Shreyas Gopal and Sanjay Raja note that EUR/GBP continues to trade above levels implied by Bank of England (BoE)-European Central Bank (ECB) rate differentials, leaving a measurable risk premium in the Pound (GBP).

articles.title: Bank of Canada statement from the April 2026 rate decision
articles.expand: The Bank of Canada today held its target for the overnight rate at 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%. The evolving conflict in the Middle East is causing heightened volatility and US trade policy continues to reshape global trade patterns. Both are ongoing sources of uncertainty. The Bank’s April outlook assumes tariff...

articles.title: Bank of Canada rate decision: Hold at 2.25% as expected
articles.expand: Prior was 2.25% BOC says "the labour market is soft" The Bank’s outlook assumes tariffs remain unchanged and the global benchmark price of oil declines to US$75 per barrel by mid 2027 China’s economy is being supported by robust exports. In the euro area, higher prices for oil and natural gas will weigh on economic activity Overall, the global econ...

articles.title: USD: Pre-FOMC demand builds – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Bob Savage notes the US Dollar (USD) is entering the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting with strengthening demand, including a first five-day net purchase streak in three months and rising use of USD cash and short-term instruments.

articles.title: Federal Reserve set to stand pat in potentially Powell’s last meeting as chair
articles.expand: The United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) announces its interest rate decision on Wednesday, another pivotal meeting for markets to gauge the stance of policymakers as energy prices remain uncomfortably high amid ongoing uncertainty in the Middle East, putting the Fed’s dual mandate under strain.

articles.title: EUR/USD Holds Steady Ahead of Fed Meeting, Focus on Middle East Outlook
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex EUR/USD is slightly lower on Wednesday, trading with minimal movement around 1.1708. The market is preparing for a Federal Reserve meeting, which could be Jerome Powell’s last before his term ends in May. The regulator is expected to keep rates unchanged. However, investors will closely monitor its assessmen...

articles.title: The USDCAD is marginally lower ahead of Bank of Canada decision. What are the key levels?
articles.expand: The Bank of Canada will announce or constrict decision at 9:45 AM. BoC expected to hold rates at 2.25%, maintaining a cautious, “wait-and-see” stance Policy balance: Weak growth and labor market vs. inflation risks from higher energy prices (US–Iran conflict) Inflation backdrop: Headline CPI rose to 2.4% (energy-driven) Core (Trimmed-Mean) eased to...

articles.title: Oil: Futures rally on Hormuz risks – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank's Senior Macro Strategist Bas van Geffen notes Brent futures have surged above $112 as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz persists and negotiations between Iran and the US remain deadlocked.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Downside bias holds as BoC interest rate decision looms
articles.expand: USD/CAD trades with a mild downside bias on Wednesday ahead of the Bank of Canada’s (BoC) interest rate decision due at 13:45 GMT.

articles.title: Fed: Policy focus and leadership change – DBS
articles.expand: Philip Wee of DBS Bank discusses the Federal Reserve’s expected decision to keep the Fed Funds Rate unchanged while Jerome Powell holds his final press conference as Chair.

articles.title: CAD: BoC hawkish tilt with conflict risks – MUFG
articles.expand: Derek Halpenny at MUFG highlights that the Canadian Dollar (CAD) has held up well versus the US Dollar since the Middle East conflict began, with markets expecting the Bank of Canada (BoC) to keep rates unchanged but adopt a more hawkish tone.

articles.title: Fed: Neutral stance into April FOMC – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ Global Strategy Team expects the Federal Reserve to keep the policy rate at 3.50–3.75% at the April FOMC meeting, describing the Committee as patient given balanced labor markets and oil-driven headline inflation.

articles.title: The USD is higher ahead of the data dump and FOMC rate decision
articles.expand: The focus today is firmly on the Federal Reserve, with the FOMC expected to leave rates unchanged when the decision is released at 2:00 PM ET. While the outcome itself is largely priced in, the significance of this meeting goes beyond the rate decision. It is expected to be the final meeting chaired by Jerome Powell, adding an element of transition...

articles.title: USD/JPY: Range trade with intervention risk – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong note USD/JPY stayed range-bound after a mildly hawkish Bank of Japan (BoJ) hold, as cautious guidance and high Oil prices erased initial Yen (JPY) gains.

articles.title: GBP/USD: Iran shock risk for UK – BNY
articles.expand: BNY's Bob Savage relays National Institute of Economic and Social Research (NIESR) analysis that an escalation of the Iran conflict and prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure could severely hit the United Kingdom (UK) economy.

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Trades sideways around 1.3500 ahead of Fed-BoE rate decisions
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair is broadly sideways around 1.3500 during the European trading session on Wednesday. The Cable consolidates as investors await monetary policy announcements by the Federal Reserve (Fed) and the Bank of England (BoE).

articles.title: USD/JPY nears the key 160.00 level ahead of the Fed rate decision
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) nudges higher for the second consecutive day against the Japanese Yen (JPY) on Wednesday, trading at 159.75 at the time of writing, with the key 160.00 level, considered a line in the sand for Tokyo intervention, coming closer.

articles.expand: The Aussie took hits after softer core inflation cooled RBA hike expectations, even as broader tightening bets stayed mostly intact.

articles.expand: Australia’s inflation accelerated sharply in March, with headline CPI rising from 3.7% yoy to 4.6% yoy, the highest since September 2023, though slightly below expectations of 4.8% yoy. articles.title: NIESR slashes UK growth forecast as Iran war and inflation hit economy
articles.expand: Britain's economy faces a sharp slowdown this year and in 2027 due to the Iran war, and inflation will stay above the Bank of England's target until 2028, according to forecasts from a leading think tank published a day before the ​BoE sets interest rates.

articles.expand: The decision itself is unlikely to move markets, with the hold unanimous and widely expected. The significance lies in the language.
